answer: Common scams in Lisbon include pickpocketing on Tram 28, overpriced tourist restaurants in Alfama, taxi scams particularly from unlicensed cabs outside the airport, and street sellers pressuring tourists near monuments. Overall Lisbon is a very safe European capital.

results.title: Lisbon Safety Guide 2026 — Scams and How to Avoid Them
results.content: Lisbon is one of Europe's safest capitals, but petty theft is a real concern in tourist areas: (1) Tram 28 pickpockets — This iconic tram is notorious for organised pickpocket gangs, especially at Graça and Alfama stops. Keep valuables in front pockets or a crossbody bag clipped shut. (2) Airport taxi scam — Unlicensed drivers outside arrivals quote fixed high fares. Only use the official taxi rank inside the terminal, or Uber. The licensed taxi from the airport to the centre costs €15–20 by meter. (3) Alfama restaurant overcharging — Some restaurants near the castle bring bread, olives, and butter to the table without asking. These 'couvert' items are charged (€2–4/person) and it's legal. Ask to remove them if you don't want to pay. (4) Street 'drug dealers' near Rossio — Don't engage with anyone offering to sell you drugs near Praça do Rossio or the metro.

results.title: Is Lisbon Safe for Solo Travellers in 2026?
results.content: Lisbon is very safe for solo travellers including solo women. The main risks are pickpocketing in tourist areas (Alfama, Baixa, Belém) and a few scams targeting tourists: (1) Currency exchange scams — A few exchange bureaux in Rossio charge extremely high commissions not visible upfront. Use ATMs or exchange at Correios (post office). (2) Fake Fado — Many 'fado houses' near the castle are tourist traps with mediocre singers and overpriced dinners. For authentic fado, go to clubs in Mouraria or the Bairro Alto that locals actually attend — ask your hotel. (3) Counterfeit goods — The Alfama flea market sells knock-off branded goods. Buying counterfeits is illegal in Portugal.

results.title: Portugal Safety Information for Indian Tourists
results.content: Indian nationals should be aware: (1) Schengen visa overstay — Portugal is a Schengen country. Ensure your visa has sufficient validity and do not overstay, as this can affect future Schengen visa applications. (2) Emergency number — 112 is the emergency number in Portugal. (3) Indian Embassy in Lisbon: Rua Pero de Alenquer 16, 1400-295 Lisboa, Tel: +351 21 301 5780. (4) Healthcare — India has a limited bilateral health agreement with Portugal. Keep travel insurance active throughout your stay.
